text: oh dear 2025 isn't getting off to a
great start is it especially if you
happen to work in Tinsel Town I mean the
first quarter of the Year generally
isn't filled with too many box offers
juggernauts January and to some extent
February are well known as dump months
where Studios shart out the malformed
runs of the litter that they don't
expect to have a chance against the big
hitters in summer movie season the
reasons for this are pretty obvious
everyone's broke after the festive
season and more concerned with losing
those beer guts than shoveling
overpriced pot popcorn down their
gullets the weather's still dark and
cold most of the time unless you're one
of those weirdos who lives in Australia
but I don't trust a single one of them
and nobody's feeling particularly
enthusiastic about dragging themselves
to the movie theater to Shell at $100 to
do something they could just as easily
do at home for free now that's fine and
all but generally by March things have
started to noticeably pick up as the
weather improves people have more money
again and bigger and more interesting
movies start to come out
really but here's the thing it's not
happening this year like at all and the
problem has been neatly summed up by
this article from The Hollywood Reporter
that describes the movie novakaine as
leading the worst weekend of the year so
far at the box office no [ __ ] I mean I
can't say I'm too surprised to be honest
we're not exactly swimming in unmissable
content right now take novakaine for
example tell me do you want to see Jack
Quaid playing a weak man who gets his
ass kicked over and over again you know
like every other role he's played for
the past 5 years Jesus I don't know what
that dude must have done to anger the
powers that be in Hollywood but it must
have been something pretty bad cuz his
entire career seems to have been one
long humiliation ritual here's a little
tip for you Hollywood we've had our fill
of weak male characters being humiliated
and beat down thank you maybe you could
try switching things up and writing male
leads that don't make us want to throw
up in our mouths then there's Mickey 17
a misfiring sci-fi comedy with one of
the worst cases of TDS outside of Mark
Hamill's living room that feels like it
was written 10 years ago cost $120
million to make and is raked in a grand
total of 90 million
worldwid or how about Captain America
Brave New World which is somehow still
hanging on in cinemas more than a month
after release probably because there's
[ __ ] all left to compete with it a movie
that cost anywhere up to $300 million
after all the re- shoots failed to even
reach 400 million worldwide and seems to
have disappeared Without a Trace after
its opening weekend becoming Marvel's
first V of the year don't worry though I
suspect it won't be the
last the only decent movie out right now
is Steven soderberg's spy Thriller black
bag with an All-Star cast and a pretty
slick screenplay which for some reason
has been lumbered with a non-existent
marketing campaign to the point where I
didn't even know the movie [ __ ]
existed until I saw it on my local
cinema listings seriously go watch it if
you can and support movies that actually
deserve it either way though none of
this is going to change the fact that
the first quarter of 2025 is shaping up
to be pretty dismal for Hollywood with a
series of flops and underperformers
failing to liven up what's already a
pretty slow period what really made me
laugh though was the idea that
Distributors are holding out hope for
Snow White to turn their fortunes
around hate to break it to you chaps but
with a projected 50 million opening
weekend and some of the worst PR in
Hollywood history something tells me
this isn't going to be the box office
savior you're hoping for so if not Snow
White then can anything jolt Cinemas
into life over the next few months well
the Minecraft movie is coming out in
April which actually seems to have
recovered from that disastrous first
trailer to build up some decent momentum
generally these days I see Jack Black
movies as the place where comedy and
entertainment go to die but the
Minecraft brand is so strong that it's
probably going to do well with kids at
least Thunderbolt seems neatly
positioned to be another Marvel dud
tonally it looks broadly in line with
Brave New World lots of clandestine
missions and government conspiracies
only without the big name stars and
recognizable characters so I've got no
idea what Marvel thinks the actual draw
is supposed to be hey want to watch a
film about a bunch of second rate
rejects from movies and TV shows you can
barely remember who aren't big enough to
support their own solo flicks didn't
think so Mission Impossible is probably
going to do well in may even though it's
coming off the back of dead reckoning if
for no other reason then it's likely Tom
Cruz's final outing in the franchise
although [ __ ] me that guy's probably
still going to be doing action flicks
when he's like 8 80 years old for some
reason all the big hitters seem to be
sandwiched into July with Superman
Fantastic 4 and Jurassic world rebirth
duking it out for box office Supremacy
and honestly I wouldn't want to take
bets on who's going to win this one the
Jurassic World movies have all been
strong performers damn man even a
[ __ ] bag of [ __ ] like Dominion
somehow topped a billion dollars so
there's a pretty strong chance that
rebirth might end up sweeping the
superhero movies aside alt together and
let's not kid ourselves if they both
flop that might to spell the end for
Cape [ __ ] movies in general which have
been declining for years by this point
three box office bombs in a row would be
definitive proof that the MCU has
finally burned through all the fan
Goodwill that it spent a decade building
up on the other hand Superman failing to
launch would be a huge blow to James
gun's attempt to relaunch DC which
already seems to be on Shaky Ground the
more I learn about it one way or another
July is going to be a maker break month
for a lot of different franchises and
while there's always a chance that two
big superhero movies released around the
same time might end up creating a barbon
Heimer effect where they build each
other up instead of cannibalizing each
other's audience I don't get the
impression that we're heading in that
direction the fact is there's just not
that much to get excited about at the
cinema this year 2025 is mostly another
year dominated by sequels and franchises
that feel increasingly tired and played
out speaking of which there's another
Avatar movie coming out in December to
round out the
year another 3-hour James Cameron Tech
demo that's probably going to rake in a
[ __ ] zillion dollars make absolutely
zero cultural impact and be forgotten
about within a matter of weeks the movie
equivalent of a fast food meal seriously
how can a guy who gave us Terminator
aliens and Titanic devote so much of his
life and career to something so [ __ ]
shallow anyway whatever if he wants to
spend the back half of his career
churning out movies that nobody's ever
going to remember I'm not going to
defend him for it the point I'm making
here is that the sluggish start of the
Year couple with the complete lack of
interest in Awards season feels like a
symptom of a larger problem in the movie
industry right now there's just no
anticipation no real excitement for
anything new no sense of a shared
cultural experience that we can't wait
to talk about there's a kind of
weariness that's settled over the film
industry these past few years an apathy
and inertia like everyone's basically
out of ideas the occasional attempts at
something new are all failing and so
they're all just waiting around for the
next big Trend to jump on the problem is
that nobody quite sure what it's going
to be when it's coming or has the balls
to actually take a risk and make it
happen so instead we're stuck in this
awkward holding pattern churning out
sequels to agan franchises just to keep
kicking the can down the road a little
while longer waiting for something or
someone to shake things up I don't know
when or if that's ever going to happen
but what I do know is that if the
Hollywood System even hopes to survive
the next few years they need to find a
solution pretty [ __ ] fast it's
Hollywood baby anyway
that's all I've got for today go away
now
